evals.prompt: The feature is complete and tested. Publish the intended local changes as a draft pull request and give me the URL.
evals.expected_output: A safely scoped branch, focused commit, and draft pull request, with validation evidence and the pull-request URL reported.
expectations: Inspects the local diff and confirms the intended scope before staging or publishing.
Uses a non-default work branch, a focused commit, and a draft pull request unless the repository state exposes a concrete blocker.
Does not sweep unrelated work into the commit or publish a ready-for-review pull request without authorization.
Returns the branch, commit, validation evidence, pull-request URL, and any real blocker.

evals.prompt: Commit these intended changes locally. Do not push the branch or open a pull request.
evals.expected_output: A handoff to the local commit workflow that creates no push, publication branch, or pull request.
expectations: Keeps the task in the local commit workflow rather than entering pull-request publication.
Does not push, create a branch solely for publication, or open or update a pull request.
Preserves unrelated working-tree changes and reports the local commit result.

evals.prompt: Update the existing draft pull request for this UI change. Its body currently contains only a text list of affected screens, while real captures are available in the workspace.
evals.expected_output: An updated draft pull request with real screenshot or clip embeds placed near the relevant explanation and verified in the live body.
expectations: Adds actual screenshot or clip embeds near the relevant explanation instead of leaving a text-only screen list.
Uses available real captures rather than placeholder URLs or invented evidence.
Re-reads the live pull-request body to confirm the embeds are present before reporting completion.
Keeps the pull request draft and names the evidence gap only when required captures are genuinely unavailable.

evals.prompt: Publish this multi-file checkout recovery change as a draft PR. Product and engineering will both review it.
evals.expected_output: A draft PR whose body explains the user-visible outcome, relevant prior behavior, core implementation idea, conceptual change groups, evidence, and review focus in language product and engineering readers can follow.
expectations: Leads with user-visible outcome and why it matters rather than a file list or commit summary.
Explains only the prior context needed to understand the change and briefly translates specialized terms.
Groups implementation by responsibility, dependency, or user flow instead of alphabetical file order.
Ends with concrete validation evidence, remaining gaps, and useful review focus.

evals.prompt: Update the draft PR for this animated navigation redesign. The workspace has a design mock, a still capture of the implementation, and a short recording of the transition.
evals.expected_output: A live PR body that clearly labels the mock, uses the real implementation capture as evidence, includes the clip for motion, and explains what each visual proves.
expectations: Labels the mock as intended design rather than implemented behavior and places it near the implementation comparison.
Uses the real still capture to prove the implemented result and the short clip to demonstrate the transition.
Provides useful alt text and product-first captions that name the state and what reviewers should notice.
Verifies that the assets render in the live PR body and keeps the PR draft if required evidence is missing.
